title: Observability Gap Sweep Loop
summary: Adds useful logs, metrics, and traces for important paths without creating noisy telemetry.
steps: List critical user and system paths, then map what evidence exists for success, failure, latency, and ownership.
Choose the highest-value missing signal and define how it will be queried during an incident.
Add structured telemetry with stable names, safe fields, and cardinality controls.
Test that the signal is emitted for success and failure cases without leaking sensitive data.
Update runbooks or dashboards so the signal is discoverable.
stopConditions: critical paths have sufficient actionable signals
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Observability Gap Sweep Loop for [service, critical_paths, signal_policy].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- List critical user and system paths, then map what evidence exists for success, failure, latency, and ownership.
- Choose the highest-value missing signal and define how it will be queried during an incident.
- Add structured telemetry with stable names, safe fields, and cardinality controls.
- Test that the signal is emitted for success and failure cases without leaking sensitive data.
- Update runbooks or dashboards so the signal is discoverable.

Quality gates before completion:
- signal answers an operational question
- sensitive fields excluded
- cardinality is bounded
- tests or local proof exist
- runbook updated

Stop when one of these is true:
- critical paths have sufficient actionable signals
- remaining gaps require platform access
- budget ends

Finish with:
- path-to-signal matrix
- sample log or metric output
- test output
- dashboard or runbook reference

Escalate for human approval before: logging customer data, changing retention policy, adding high-volume metrics.
